entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
John Carvalho, CEO of Synonym.to, firmly addresses concerns regarding the potential for inflating Bitcoin by transferring or dividing satoshis onto another network. He emphasizes that such actions are not technically feasible without resorting to a significant protocol alteration, commonly known as a hard fork. Carvalho dismisses the notion of creating inflation within the Bitcoin network as both absurd and a misunderstanding of the fundamental economic principles underpinning Bitcoin.
Carvalho further clarifies that Bitcoin's design inherently counters any theoretical supply increases through its proof of work and difficulty adjustment mechanisms. As demand for block space rises, transaction fees increase, which in turn encourages more miners to participate, thus enhancing the network's security. He analogizes misguided inflationary arguments with the paradoxical complaint that a restaurant is too popular to attract customers, illustrating the self-regulating nature of Bitcoin’s economy.
He advocates for a greater appreciation of the insights provided by Eric, who has also elaborated on these issues, suggesting that community members should focus more on understanding the existing robust mechanisms of Bitcoin rather than proposing unnecessary fixes to a system that is not broken. For further details, John Carvalho can be reached through his company's website at [Synonym.to](http://synonym.to/).
